title: ulmus carpinifolia var. dampieri
intro: Ulmus carpinifolia var. dampieri, also known as Dampier's wych elm, is a variety of the wych elm tree native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of promoting gut health, reducing inflammation, and soothing the skin. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
